name: bc-symbols-downloader
description: Download Business Central symbol packages listed in app.json from NuGet feeds (public and private) into .alpackages.

bcSymbols.useDefaultFeeds.markdownDescription: Include the public Microsoft (`MSSymbolsV2`) and AppSource (`AppSourceSymbols`) feeds in addition to any feeds configured in `#bcSymbols.feeds#`.

bcSymbols.feeds.markdownDescription: Additional NuGet v3 feeds to search for symbol packages. Feeds are searched in order, after the default public feeds.

name.description: Display name used in prompts and when storing credentials.

url.description: The NuGet v3 service index URL (usually ends with /nuget/v3/index.json).

enumDescriptions: Azure DevOps style Personal Access Token (sent as HTTP Basic password).
HTTP Basic authentication with username and password.
authentication.description: How to authenticate against this feed. Credentials are stored in the VS Code Secret Storage, never in settings.

bcSymbols.targetDirectory.description: Directory (relative to the app.json folder) where the .app symbol files are written.

bcSymbols.includeApplicationAndPlatform.markdownDescription: Also download the Microsoft first-party foundational symbols — `Platform` (System), `Application`, `System Application`, `Base Application` and `Business Foundation` — matching the `application` version in app.json, not just the entries under `dependencies`.

bcSymbols.countryCode.markdownDescription: Localization used for the country-specific Microsoft symbols (`Application` and `Base Application`). Use `w1` (or leave empty) for the base/W1 packages, or a country code such as `de`, `us`, `gb`. `System Application` and `Business Foundation` are always W1.
